title: [security] non-constant-time pubkey comparison in coap_keys.c TOFU
description: In lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_keys.c:412, lichen_key_store_put() uses regular memcmp() on 32-byte pubkey for TOFU pinning check. This creates a timing side-channel. Project already has public_key_ct_compare() in replay.c:27-36 for exactly this purpose (constant-time XOR diff). Also affects find_key_locked() on IIDs at line 370. Matches reviewer focus on key handling + crypto timing. OSCORE/EDHOC/Schnorr/MIC not directly touched in last 20 commits (mostly bd auto-commits + one CBOR fix in coap_keys). No obvious issues in Schnorr48 verify or OSCORE ctx from quick scan of recent merges.
close_reason: Replaced memcmp() with key_ct_compare() (volatile uint8_t XOR accumulation) in coap_keys.c:379 (IID lookup) and :421 (TOFU pubkey pin). Matches replay.c pattern. Verified via coap_keys ztest (TOFU pinning test passes). Commit 3f829f65a
